title: Camissonia kernensis
intro: Camissonia kernensis, also known as Kern marsh marigold, is a rare, annual plant species native to the southwestern United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. Camissonia kernensis contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Camissonia kernensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. This classification places it within the genus Camissonia, which consists of a group of annual herbs native to western North America. Camissonia kernensis is further classified as Camissonia kernensis (Eastw.) P.H. Thomson, indicating its specific name and authority.
